title: Eurybia glauca
intro: Eurybia glauca, also known as Low Blueberry or Blue Mountain Blueberry, is a perennial plant species in the Aster family, characterized by its small, blue-purple berries and deciduous foliage. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its antioxidant properties, which may help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Eurybia glauca include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and anthocyanins, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Eurybia glauca is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to support cardiovascular health and immune function.
taxonomy: Eurybia glauca is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to the northern hemisphere, particularly found in areas with cold and moist climates. Eurybia glauca is a member of the genus Eurybia, which is a group of plants commonly known as the mountain aster or wood's aster. The plant is a perennial herb that produces small, white or purple flowers. Eurybia glauca is also known as the glaucous aster or Eurybia.
uses: Eurybia glauca, also known as the northern kelp or glaucous eurybia, is a species of seaweed that has been utilized for its medicinal properties.
